intent: Write a capstone 3-source report on 'the realist's guide to smart-home starter upgrades' capped at $250. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse the electronics, home-kitchen, and health-household categories and collect at least 5 smart-home-adjacent products (smart plug, bulb, sensor, speaker, small appliance). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/technology AND /f/LifeProTips for posts on which smart-home devices deliver value versus which become junk. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Home automation' and 'Internet of things' articles and extract 3-4 facts about protocols (Zigbee, Matter), privacy concerns, and historical adoption. The report must: (a) propose a $250 starter bundle with running totals, (b) justify each pick with Wikipedia-grounded protocol/privacy reasoning, (c) apply community guardrails (e.g. 'avoid brand lock-in'), (d) close with a three-year outlook on which purchases will age well. Cite every claim with a markdown link.
author_notes: Hard 3-site capstone. Smart-home starter with protocol grounding + longevity outlook.
